purpose.summary: The DBUNLDGS program extracts pending authorization summary and detail information from an IMS database and writes it to a GSAM file. It reads authorization summary records and then retrieves associated detail records, writing both to the GSAM file.

inputs.name: PAUTBPCB
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Summary IMS database PCB

inputs.name: PADFLPCB
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Detail IMS database PCB

outputs.name: GSAM Output
outputs.description: GSAM output file containing parent and child segment records.

called_programs.purpose: Issue IMS DL/I calls to retrieve and insert data.
Issue IMS DL/I calls to retrieve and insert data.
Issue IMS DL/I calls to insert data into GSAM file.

transforms.transformation_description: Moves the account ID from the pending authorization summary record to the root segment key.

paragraphs.purpose: This is the program identifier paragraph. It does not contain any executable code. It simply marks the beginning of the program.

This paragraph serves as the main control loop for the program. It first performs 1000-INITIALIZE to set up the program environment. Then, it repeatedly performs 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY until WS-END-OF-ROOT-SEG is set to 'Y', indicating the end of the authorization summary data. Finally, it performs 4000-FILE-CLOSE to close the output file before terminating the program. The paragraph uses the linkage section to receive the PCBs.

This paragraph initializes the program environment. It accepts the current date and day from the system. It then displays a series of messages to the console, including the program name and the current date. This paragraph prepares the program for processing by setting up initial values and providing informational output.
This paragraph simply contains the EXIT statement, marking the end of the 1000-INITIALIZE paragraph. It is used to define the scope of the PERFORM THRU statement in MAIN-PARA.

This paragraph retrieves the next pending authorization summary record from the IMS database. It initializes the PAUT-PCB-STATUS and then calls CBLTDLI with the FUNC-GN parameter to retrieve the next root segment. If the PAUT-PCB-STATUS is spaces, it means a record was successfully read, and the program increments counters, moves the data to OPFIL1-REC, initializes ROOT-SEG-KEY and CHILD-SEG-REC, moves PA-ACCT-ID to ROOT-SEG-KEY, and then performs 3100-INSERT-PARENT-SEG-GSAM to write the parent segment to the GSAM file and then calls 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL to process child segments. If the PAUT-PCB-STATUS is 'GB', it sets the end-of-file flag. If the PAUT-PCB-STATUS is neither spaces nor 'GB', it displays an error message and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program.
This paragraph simply contains the EXIT statement, marking the end of the 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY paragraph. It is used to define the scope of the PERFORM THRU statement in MAIN-PARA.

This paragraph retrieves the next pending authorization detail record from the IMS database. It calls CBLTDLI with the FUNC-GNP parameter to retrieve the next child segment. If the PAUT-PCB-STATUS is spaces, it means a record was successfully read, and the program sets MORE-AUTHS to TRUE, increments counters, moves the data to CHILD-SEG-REC, and performs 3200-INSERT-CHILD-SEG-GSAM to write the child segment to the GSAM file. If the PAUT-PCB-STATUS is 'GE', it sets the end-of-child-segment flag. If the PAUT-PCB-STATUS is neither spaces nor 'GE', it displays an error message and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. Finally, it initializes PAUT-PCB-STATUS.

This paragraph simply contains the EXIT statement, marking the end of the 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL paragraph. It is used to define the scope of the PERFORM THRU statement in 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY.

This paragraph inserts the parent segment (PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY) into the GSAM file. It calls CBLTDLI with the FUNC-ISRT parameter to perform the insert. If the PASFL-PCB-STATUS is not spaces, indicating an error, it displays an error message and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program.

This paragraph simply contains the EXIT statement, marking the end of the 3100-INSERT-PARENT-SEG-GSAM paragraph. It is used to define the scope of the PERFORM THRU statement in 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY.

error_handling.condition: PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'GB'
PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'GE'

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of the IMSFUNCS, CIPAUSMY, CIPAUDTY, PAUTBPCB, PASFLPCB, and PADFLPCB calls in the DBUNLDGS paragraph?
open_questions.context: The code only shows the paragraph name and the calls, but not the purpose of these calls.
open_questions.suggestion: Examine the definitions of these called programs or functions to determine their purpose.

open_questions.question: What is the structure of the GSAM output file?
open_questions.context: The code writes parent and child segments to the GSAM file, but the specific format and content of the file are not clear.
open_questions.suggestion: Examine the copybooks or data definitions associated with the GSAM file to determine its structure.
